name: Dementia - Medication-Only Patients (No Diagnosis)
description: Tricky test case: finds patients on cognitive enhancer medications but with NO dementia diagnosis on the problem list (Path C). The PheKB algorithm explicitly allows this — a single dementia drug fill is sufficient. Real-world scenario: patient evaluated by outside neurologist who started donepezil empirically; the formal dementia diagnosis was never coded in the local EHR. Discoverable only by searching MedicationRequest.
prompt: I want to identify patients in our system who are taking dementia medications but don't have a formal dementia diagnosis on their problem list. These may be patients diagnosed elsewhere or where the diagnosis hasn't been formally documented yet.

prompts.naive: Find patients on dementia medications who don't have a dementia diagnosis.
prompts.expert: I need to find a documentation gap: patients on cognitive enhancer medications (cholinesterase inhibitors or NMDA antagonists) who lack a formal dementia Condition resource. Two-step approach: first find all patients with MedicationRequest for dementia drugs (donepezil 997223 and 1100184, galantamine 310436, memantine 996740, donepezil/memantine combo 1599803), then exclude those with a Condition for dementia subtypes (26929004 Alzheimer's, 429998004 Vascular, 312991009 Lewy body, 230270009 Frontotemporal, 52448006 Dementia).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C 'tricky' patients: have cognitive enhancer meds but NO dementia Condition resource. Tests cross-resource reasoning — the LLM must search MedicationRequest and recognize that the PheKB algorithm explicitly allows medication-only identification.

required_codes.display: 24 HR Donepezil 10 MG / Memantine 28 MG ER Oral Capsule
